issues.description: The `reject` and `evidence` modes each get their own if-block but render the exact same component (`SellerNoteInput`) with identical props. If a third note-based mode is added, another if-block will be required. The mode branching could be collapsed so `reject` and `evidence` share a single branch since they differ only by action name.
issues.fix: Consolidate the two identical `SellerNoteInput` branches into one: `if (mode === 'reject' || mode === 'evidence')` — this removes the duplication and avoids future OCP violations when new note-based modes are added.
issues.codeSnippet:
// mode === 'reject' and mode === 'evidence' both render:
<SellerNoteInput onSubmit={handleNoteSubmit} onCancel={() => setMode(null)} isPending={isPending} placeholder={t('notePlaceholder')} />

issues.description: `UserTableProps` has 14 props covering filter state, pagination, selection, and export capability. This is a wide interface that bundles multiple distinct concerns (filtering, paging, selection, export) into a single component boundary. The `renderTableBody` standalone function also receives 7 positional parameters, which is a symptom of the same problem.
issues.fix: Consider splitting into composable sub-components or context: a `UserFiltersBar` (filterInput, roleFilter, itemFilter, canExport, onExportCsv) and a `UserTableBody` accepting only data + selection props. Under KISS, if the split would add unnecessary indirection, keep as-is and document intentionally — but the 14-prop interface is worth noting.

issues.description: The hook comment says it 'encapsulates all data-fetching, mutations, and UI state' — which is exactly the SRP violation. It combines: (1) data fetching via `usePaymentMethods`, (2) three mutation operations (create, update, delete), (3) UI state management (expandedId), and (4) sort-order swap logic (handleMoveUp / handleMoveDown). Additionally, `handleDelete` calls `globalThis.confirm()`, mixing browser dialog UI directly into application-layer logic.
issues.fix: Under KISS, the orchestration is acceptable for a page-level hook, but `globalThis.confirm()` in an application hook is the clearest violation — it ties the hook to browser UI. Move the confirm dialog to the presentation layer (component) so the hook only receives a 'delete confirmed' callback. The sort-swap logic (handleMoveUp/handleMoveDown) could also be extracted to a pure utility.

issues.description: The `FileInput` private helper calls `alert()` directly to show an error when a file exceeds the size limit. The parent component already has an `error` prop that is designed for this purpose. Using `alert()` in a presentation component bypasses the established error-display contract and makes the component untestable in unit tests.
issues.fix:
Instead of calling `alert()`, invoke the `onChange('')` path and have the parent pass the error message down via the `error` prop. The component already has error display rendering: `{error &&

{error}

}`. Alternatively, add an `onFileError` callback prop.

issues.description: At 394 lines, `InlineImageCarousel` handles: drag-and-drop reorder logic, broken-image tracking state, active index management, thumbnail rendering (desktop + mobile), main image rendering (with two separate layouts), inline edit bar rendering, add/remove/cover-set operations. Each of these is a distinct responsibility. The component is not a 'god component' by itself but the desktop-vs-mobile dual rendering adds significant duplication and complexity.
issues.fix: The most impactful extract would be the thumbnail strip (desktop vertical / mobile horizontal) into a shared `ImageThumbnailStrip` component receiving `direction` prop — this would eliminate the near-duplicate thumbnail rendering blocks. This is a KISS/SRP tradeoff; if the layouts are expected to diverge, keep them separate.
issues.codeSnippet:
// Duplicate thumbnail rendering blocks at lines 332-361 (desktop) and 376-386 (mobile)
// Both call renderThumbContent(field, index) but with different wrapper layouts

patterns.pattern: globalThis.confirm() used in application hooks and presentation components
patterns.recommendation: Consider creating a shared confirm dialog or use the existing inline confirm pattern (already used in ProductTableRow) for consistency and testability.

patterns.pattern: No direct API calls (fetch/axios/supabase) in presentation components
patterns.recommendation: Good pattern observed: all data access goes through application-layer hooks. No DIP violations of this type were found.

patterns.pattern: Large prop interfaces on table/list components
patterns.recommendation: Table components with many props are common in admin UIs. Acceptable under KISS, but ISP suggests grouping related props (filter state, selection state) into typed sub-objects.
